summary: Tooth eruption begins at Stage 6 of Nolla’s classification because it marks the first appearance of the crown above the gingival margin. Prior stages involve root formation within the socket without any crown exposure or alveolar displacement. Root growth alone does not equate to eruption; only the physical break-through of the crown constitutes true eruption according to standard dental definitions. Thus, Stage 6 is the definitive point when eruption commences clinically and anatomically.
